title: GitHub Copilot — AI pair programmer
snippet: GitHub Copilot helps more than 1 million developers. Market leader with ~37% share. 30M+ users claimed. ChatGPT (82%) and GitHub Copilot (68%) are the most used AI tools per Stack Overflow 2025 survey.

metadata.llm_reason: Core market leader in AI coding tools

title: Cursor — AI-native code editor
snippet: VS Code fork rebuilt around AI. ARR exceeded $2B by Feb 2026. Raised $3.2B in 2025 across two rounds. $9B valuation. Scored 9.2 in comparative testing. Over $500M ARR. Fastest-growing software company ever.

metadata.llm_reason: Leading AI-native IDE with explosive growth

title: Claude Code — Anthropic's CLI coding agent
snippet: Terminal-based AI coding tool. Run-rate revenue over $2.5B as of March 2026, doubling since beginning of 2026. Most used AI programming tool in developer surveys within 10 months of launch.

metadata.llm_reason: Major commercial player with rapid growth

title: StarCoder2 — open-source code LLM by BigCode
snippet: Leading open-source code model from BigCode project (ServiceNow + HuggingFace). Multiple sizes. Foundational for many downstream code models.

metadata.llm_reason: Key open-source code model

title: DeepSeek-Coder-V2 — MoE code model
snippet: DeepSeek's code model. 671B parameters MoE architecture. API costs ~15x lower than Claude/GPT-4o. Competitive with frontier models on coding benchmarks.

metadata.llm_reason: Leading open-source code model with cost advantage

title: Devin — AI software engineer by Cognition Labs
snippet: First AI software engineer. Demonstrated autonomous software development capabilities. Sparked the agentic coding movement.

metadata.llm_reason: Pioneering AI software engineer agent

title: Aider — open-source AI pair programmer
snippet: CLI-based AI pair programmer. Works with any LLM. Popular among developers who prefer terminal-based workflows.

metadata.llm_reason: Leading open-source CLI coding tool

title: OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin) — open-source AI developer
snippet: The most popular open-source AI agent. AI-Driven Development platform. Open-source alternative to Devin.

metadata.llm_reason: Top open-source AI coding agent

title: SWE-bench — real-world GitHub issue benchmark
snippet: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues? The dominant benchmark for AI coding agents. Multiple variants: Verified, Pro, Lite.

metadata.llm_reason: The primary benchmark for coding agents

title: HumanEval — code generation benchmark
snippet: 164 Python programming problems. Near-solved by frontier models (Kimi K2.5 scores 99.0). Foundational but now considered too easy.

metadata.llm_reason: Foundational code generation benchmark, now near-saturated

title: LiveCodeBench — contamination-free coding benchmark
snippet: Continuously harvests fresh competitive programming problems from LeetCode, AtCoder, CodeForces. Top model: Gemini 3 Pro Preview at 91.7%, DeepSeek V3.2 Speciale at 89.6%. 201 models evaluated.

metadata.llm_reason: Leading contamination-free coding benchmark with live leaderboard

title: SWE-Bench++: Scalable Generation of SE Benchmarks
snippet: Automated framework generating repo-level coding tasks from open-source GitHub projects. 11,133 instances from 3,971 repos across 11 languages. Covers bug fixes and feature requests.

metadata.llm_reason: Major new benchmark framework scaling beyond SWE-bench

title: SWE-bench Goes Live! — continuously updatable benchmark
snippet: First automatically constructed, continuously updatable benchmark. 1,319 task instances from Jan 2024 to Apr 2025, spanning 93 real-world repositories.

metadata.llm_reason: Live benchmark addressing contamination concerns

title: SWE-Bench Pro: Long-Horizon Software Engineering Tasks
snippet: Substantially more challenging benchmark for realistic, complex, enterprise-level problems. GPT-5.2-Codex and Claude 4.5 score over 55%.

metadata.llm_reason: Enterprise-level coding benchmark pushing beyond SWE-bench

title: SWE-EVO: Benchmarking Coding Agents in Multi-Step Evolution
snippet: Evaluates agents on multi-step software evolution tasks spanning multiple commits requiring long-horizon planning.

metadata.llm_reason: Novel benchmark for multi-commit software evolution

title: SWE-Next: Scalable Real-World Software Engineering Tasks for Agents
snippet: Next-generation benchmark for real-world software engineering tasks. Published March 2026.

metadata.llm_reason: Latest SWE-bench successor from 2026

title: RefactorBench: Evaluating Stateful Reasoning in Language Agents Through Code
snippet: 100 handcrafted multi-file refactoring tasks from 9 open-source Python repos. Current LM agents solve only 22% vs human 87%.

metadata.llm_reason: Benchmark exposing large gap between AI and human refactoring ability

title: BigCodeBench: Diverse Function Calls and Complex Instructions
snippet: 1,140 rich-context programming tasks covering 723 function calls from 139 libraries across 7 domains. Published at ICLR 2025.

metadata.llm_reason: Major ICLR 2025 benchmark for complex code generation

title: The SWE-Bench Illusion: When LLMs Remember Instead of Reason
